mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered observations, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.5% annually, INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns, with a negative Rank IC observed over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate combined with stable or falling unemployment indicates a soft landing monetary policy environment, which sustains corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while UNRATE remains flat or falls
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL trends in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero when adjusted for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and declines in retail sales indicate stagflationary pressure that erodes real consumer purchasing power and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS is included as a control variable.
